Illustrative build of the base device.

Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned
a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke)
A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge**
Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).

rules.intent: Define the canonical icon-callout proof-point device.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout is a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) accent-shape box holding a short Brand Blue (#00529b) headline in Nunito Sans weight 800 (~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, vertically centered), with a White (#ffffff) rounded-square icon badge (line icon in Brand Blue stroke) straddling the box's left edge. It is used for supporting proof-point statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). Copy must never slide under the badge.

quotes: Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge**
Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).
keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`**
the badge's outer half has room
the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector ? 50% over the blue, 50% over the page background
Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**.

rules.intent: Guarantee the exact half-on/half-off badge-on-edge geometry.
rules.rule_text: The badge straddles the box's left edge with an exact 50/50 split: with default badge W = 80px (inner icon 48px), inset the blue box from the left by W/2 = 40px (spacing.icon_callout.inset_40) and place the badge center exactly on the box left edge (distance from badge center to box edge must be 0). Vertically center the badge via top:50% + margin-top:-H/2 (40px). Box left-padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap = W/2 + 24px. At non-default badge sizes keep these relationships (inset = W/2, left-padding = W/2 + 24px, margin-top = -H/2) so the bisector stays exact.

quotes: box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR)
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):**
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Preserve the signature asymmetric accent-shape corners.
rules.rule_text: The corner radii are mandatory and never change: box border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL) — large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio; the white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale with 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity)
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow**
This shadow is required on every instance

rules.intent: Ensure the badge reads as lifted, not pasted-on.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow so it lifts off the blue box: box-shadow 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18) — Dark Navy (#011E45) at 18% opacity. This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

quotes: **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores**
the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG**
every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical

rules.intent: Preserve fidelity of the badge treatment across email clients.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 overhang relies on position:absolute, which Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores — there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, the brand-safe option is to pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge); every other rule (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

quotes: <mj-section background-color="#ffffff" padding="16px 25px">

rules.intent: Provide the canonical build reference for the device.
rules.rule_text: Reference MJML for the icon callout: wrapper padding-left = W/2 (40px) insets the blue box; the badge is absolutely positioned (left:0 + wrapper pad) with its center on the box left edge, vertically centered; the Light Blue box uses border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px and padding 26px 34px 26px 64px; the badge is an 80px white square with radius 20px 4px 20px 4px and box-shadow 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18) holding a 48px icon.

quotes: <td style="background-color:#E6F0F9; border-radius:40px 8px 40px 8px; padding:26px 34px 26px 64px;">

<div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18); text-align:center; line-height:80px;">
